title: Skill: okf-index — OKF knowledge base builder (Confluence/web/docs/notes → Obsidian vault + FTS5 search)
description:
## Goal

Build a self-contained Pi/Codex agent skill `okf-index` that ingests knowledge from multiple sources (Confluence, websites, local documents, own notes) into an [Open Knowledge Format v0.1](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/knowledge-catalog/tree/main/okf) bundle stored as plain markdown, browsable directly in Obsidian, with fast local search (SQLite FTS5 + tag facets) and always-on LLM enrichment. Telegram sources are explicitly Phase 2.

## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Skill lives at `okf-index/` IN THIS REPO (the working skills repo) and follows THIS REPO's `AGENTS.md`: short `SKILL.md` router + `references/` + `scripts/run.py` + `config/settings.json` + `evals/evals.json`; portable `uv run --script scripts/run.py`; `schema get --json`; semantic exit codes 0-5; `--help` shows current time; help that gives the agent time context.
- Produces OKF v0.1-conformant bundles: every concept is markdown + parseable YAML frontmatter with a non-empty `type`; supports `index.md` (progressive disclosure, bundle-root may carry `okf_version`), `log.md`, bundle-relative cross-links `/path/to/x.md`, and `# Citations`. Permissive consumption (never reject on unknown fields/types).
- Bundle = a plain directory that opens directly as an Obsidian vault; no proprietary format or required Obsidian plugins for basic browsing.
- CLI topology is noun-first `resource [subresource] action` with isolated connector resources: `confluence page ...`, `web crawl|fetch`, `doc ingest`, `note add`; plus `bundle build|index|validate|stats`, `search`, `tag list|show`; `auth status|setup|check|delete`; `doctor`; `schema get`.
- Confluence integration uses an embedded REST client inside `okf-index` (fully self-contained), with its own credentials in `config/settings.json` (keyring-first, env fallback) — NOT a subprocess call to any external/sibling confluence skill.
- Web ingestion uses `crawl4ai` (`AsyncWebCrawler` + `BFSDeepCrawlStrategy` + `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`) with bounded `--max-depth`/`--max-pages` and an `--allowed-host` same-domain filter. (The usage pattern is borrowed as READ-ONLY inspiration from an external example skill; the code is implemented inside `okf-index` — no link to that skill.)
- Local document ingestion uses `markitdown` to convert `.md/.txt/.pdf/.docx/.pptx/.html` to markdown uniformly.
- Every ingest runs an LLM enrichment pass (configured model) that produces `description`, `tags[]`, and cross-link suggestions; enrichment is the default path, not optional.
- Fast search via a local SQLite FTS5 index at `<vault>/.okf/index.db` over title+description+body+tags, plus a tags table for faceting; incremental rebuild keyed on `source_id+content_hash+mtime`. `.okf/` is gitignored.
- Bundle location is the `OKF_VAULT_PATH` setting (default `~/okf-vault`). The skill NEVER auto-inits or auto-commits git; it only writes files and may print a `git status` hint.
- All reads are bounded (`--limit`, `--fields`, filters); all mutations support `--dry-run --json` then `--yes --json`.

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] `uv run --script scripts/run.py schema get --json` returns a complete, valid schema for every implemented command.
- [ ] `okf-index doctor --json` validates settings, checks keyring/env sources (no values leaked), probes Confluence + enrich model, with semantic exit codes.
- [ ] End-to-end: ingesting a Confluence page, a web page, a local `.docx`, and a note each yields an OKF-conformant concept file (frontmatter + `type`) inside `OKF_VAULT_PATH`, with LLM-generated description/tags.
- [ ] `bundle validate --json` confirms OKF v0.1 conformance (SPEC §9) for the produced bundle.
- [ ] `search "<q>" --tag <t> --type <t> --limit N --json` returns ranked, bounded results from the FTS5 index in <100ms for a 1k-concept bundle.
- [ ] `tag list --json` and `tag show <tag> --json` work off the tags table.
- [ ] `bundle index --rebuild|--since` performs incremental reindexing correctly.
- [ ] The bundle directory opens in Obsidian as a normal vault with working internal links and tag pane.
- [ ] `auth status|setup|check|delete` lifecycle works for Confluence + model credentials; `auth status` never prints secret values.
- [ ] `evals/evals.json` has atomic evals for every implemented command; enrich-related evals assert STRUCTURE (frontmatter present, `type` set, conformance), not exact LLM wording.
- [ ] All unit tests + `python3 -m py_compile` green; no secrets in repo; no `curl`/direct HTTP outside the CLI.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO direct HTTP/curl outside `scripts/run.py` (AGENTS.md: all logic via the CLI).
- ❌ NO storing tokens/model keys in the skill folder or `settings.json` (credentials: keyring → env only).
- ❌ NO non-conformant OKF output: a concept without frontmatter or without `type` is a release blocker (SPEC §9).
- ❌ NO subprocess calls to any external/sibling confluence skill (Q2 decision: embedded client).
- ❌ NO auto-init/auto-commit of git by the skill (Q4 decision: git is manual; skill only writes files).
- ❌ NO unbounded search/list (every read bounded by `--limit`/filters).
- ❌ NO hardcoded LLM provider/model (Q3: configured via `config/settings.json`).
- ❌ NO silent LLM calls without a configured provider surfaced in `doctor`.
- ❌ NO mocking OKF conformance in `bundle validate` tests (must assert real SPEC §9 rules).
- ❌ NO rewriting the FTS5 choice (Q1: SQLite FTS5 + tags is the search core).
- ❌ NO references/links/imports/subprocess/hardcoded paths to the external example-skills repo (`.../AI/skills`) or ANY sibling skill. Those repos are READ-ONLY pattern inspiration ONLY (Confluence-client approach, crawl4ai usage, skill structure). Needed logic MUST be copied into `okf-index`; the skill must run standalone with zero dependency on any other skill's files.

## Approach

Noun-first CLI with isolated connector resources (`confluence`, `web`, `doc`, `note`) feeding a shared OKF pipeline: `connector → markdown → OKF writer (frontmatter+body) → enrich (LLM: description/tags/links) → write .md + update index.md/log.md → FTS5 indexer → search`. The pipeline is shared so adding a source (e.g. Telegram in Phase 2) is one connector module + one `ingest` mapping, not a new vertical. Settings/auth/doctor reuse the AGENTS.md unified-manifest pattern. Enrichment is always-on via a configured OpenAI-compatible or Gemini model; evals assert structure, not LLM prose.

## Architecture

- `scripts/run.py` — single PEP 723 launcher (deps: crawl4ai, playwright, markitdown, keyring, google-genai or openai/litellm). Layers: settings/auth → connectors → OKF writer/validator → enrich → indexer → search.
- `config/settings.json` — manifest: `OKF_VAULT_PATH` (non-secret default), `OKF_ENRICH_PROVIDER`/`OKF_ENRICH_MODEL` (non-secret), `GEMINI_API_KEY`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` (credential), `CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL` (non-secret), `CONFLUENCE_USERNAME`+`CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN` (credential).
- Bundle layout under `OKF_VAULT_PATH`: `<source>/<concept>.md`, auto `index.md` per dir, root `log.md`, `.okf/index.db` (gitignored).
- Frontmatter producer keys beyond SPEC: `source`, `source_id`, `content_hash`, `okf_version`.

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Knowledge is scattered across Confluence, websites, local docs, and personal notes with no unified, portable, searchable, agent-friendly store. OKF v0.1 gives a vendor-neutral markdown+frontmatter standard; Obsidian already speaks that format. The gap is a producer+consumer agent skill that fills an OKF bundle from many sources and searches it fast.

### Research Findings
**OKF (SPEC v0.1):**
- Concept = markdown + YAML frontmatter; `type` REQUIRED; recommended `title, description, resource, tags, timestamp`.
- `index.md` (no frontmatter except bundle-root `okf_version`), `log.md` (ISO date sections), bundle-relative links `/path/x.md`, `# Citations`.
- Permissive: consumers MUST NOT reject on unknown types/fields/broken links (SPEC §9). Conformance = parseable frontmatter + non-empty `type` + reserved-filename rules.

**confluence skill (external example, READ-ONLY inspiration):** `uv run --script scripts/run.py`, `page get/search/section/toc/tree/attachment/diagram`, bearer auth keyring→env, `--dry-run/--yes`, `CONFLUENCE_AUTO_CONFIRM`. (We do NOT call or import it; we implement an equivalent client inside `okf-index`.)

**sandbox + crawl4ai (external example, READ-ONLY inspiration):** `AsyncWebCrawler`+`BrowserConfig`+`CrawlerRunConfig`+`BFSDeepCrawlStrategy`+`FilterChain(URLPatternFilter,ContentTypeFilter)`, `DefaultMarkdownGenerator`→markdown, playwright chromium, deep crawl with `max_depth`/`include_external=False`. (Usage pattern borrowed by reading; implementation lives inside `okf-index`.)

**AGENTS.md (THIS REPO — authoritative rules source):** SKILL.md 50-100 line router; references/runtime|safety|workflows|api; `schema get --json`; `auth status/setup/check/delete`; atomic `doctor`; unified `config/settings.json` manifest (keyring for credentials, env for non-secret overrides); `evals/evals.json`; semantic exit codes; help with current time. NOTE: other repos carry their own (possibly older) copy of AGENTS.md — always follow THIS repo's copy.

**Environment checks:** system Python ships SQLite WITH FTS5 (verified); `markitdown==0.1.6` on PyPI; beads_rust `br` is the issue tracker.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Source-connector resources + shared OKF pipeline (CHOSEN) ✓
Isolated `confluence`/`web`/`doc`/`note` resources feeding one shared writer/enricher/indexer. Noun-first, explorable, matches AGENTS.md. Adding sources = one module. Chosen for consistency with sibling skills and clean schema/help.

#### 2. Unified `ingest --from <source>` ❌
One `ingest` verb with `--from`. Rejected: mixes very different argument shapes behind one verb, weaker schema/help, harder to bound per-source.

#### 3. Declarative `source add` + `bundle build` from a manifest ❌
Reproducible `sources.yaml`, but adds indirection unneeded for interactive agent use. Reuse the idea later as an optional recipe layer, not the core.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope (Phase 1):** Confluence (embedded client), web (crawl4ai), local docs (markitdown md/txt/pdf/docx/pptx/html), own notes; OKF writer/validator; always-on LLM enrich; FTS5 index + tag facets + search; auth/doctor/settings; evals; Obsidian-compatible output.
**Out of scope (Phase 2+):** Telegram; declarative sources manifest; OKF `viz.html` graph viewer; vector/semantic search; account-linking across sources.

### Open Questions
- Exact Gemini vs OpenAI-compatible default for enrich (decide in Task 2; default Gemini to mirror OKF reference_agent).
- Concept path/slug conventions per source (decide in Task 1 core).
- Enrichment prompt + how to present suggested links for human approval (decide in Task 2).

title: Task 1: Scaffold + OKF core (writer/validator/FTS5/search) + mechanical md/txt & note ingest
description:
## Goal

Establish the `okf-index` skill scaffold and the shared OKF core engine end-to-end with the simplest sources (own notes + `.md/.txt` files), proving the full vertical slice — write OKF → validate → FTS5 index → search — with NO external network/model dependencies. This sets CLI, settings, auth, and OKF conventions that later tasks (Confluence client, web/crawl4ai, markitdown office formats, LLM enrichment) plug into.

## Effort Estimate

~12-14 hours (mechanical core, mirrors confluence/sandbox patterns, no network/model deps; pytest depth is representative-not-exhaustive for the stub-only enrich path).

## Implementation

Study first (READ-ONLY inspiration — copy patterns into `okf-index`, NEVER link/import/call):
- THIS REPO's `AGENTS.md` (authoritative rules) — `resource action`, `schema get --json`, auth lifecycle, doctor, settings manifest, exit codes 0-5, help-with-time, evals rules.
- External example skills (read-only, for patterns only): a confluence-style skill for the Confluence-client approach (page get/search/section/tree, bearer auth) and a sandbox-style skill for crawl4ai usage (`AsyncWebCrawler`+`BFSDeepCrawlStrategy`). Do NOT hardcode their paths or import them — reimplement inside `okf-index`.
- OKF SPEC v0.1 §4 (frontmatter), §6 (index.md), §7 (log.md), §9 (conformance).

### 1. Scaffold (TDD — failing tests first)
Files:
- `okf-index/SKILL.md` (50-100 line router: title, launcher, Required behavior, Decision tree, Read-when-needed).
- `okf-index/scripts/run.py` — single PEP 723 launcher; argparse noun-first; `schema get --json`; `--help` prints `Current time (<IANA>): <ISO-8601> · Unix: <ts>` at top/resource/leaf (clock injectable for tests; NOT in JSON stdout). JSON envelope: stdout `{success:true,data:...}`; stderr `{success:false,error:{code,message,hint?,retriable}}`.
- `okf-index/config/settings.json` — unified manifest.
- `okf-index/references/runtime.md`, `references/safety.md`, `references/okf.md`.
- `okf-index/evals/evals.json`, `okf-index/logo.svg`.

### 2. Settings + auth + doctor
`config/settings.json` entries (`credential` per AGENTS.md):
- `OKF_VAULT_PATH` (string, required, credential:false, default `~/okf-vault`).
- `OKF_ENRICH_PROVIDER` (credential:false, default `gemini`), `OKF_ENRICH_MODEL` (credential:false, default `""`).
- `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY` (credential:true, secret:true).
- `CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL` (credential:false), `CONFLUENCE_USERNAME` (credential:true, secret:false), `CONFLUENCE_API_TOKEN` (credential:true, secret:true).
- `keyring_service`: `earendil.okf-index`.

Loader: env overrides NON-SECRET defaults only; credentials keyring→env, never copied to `os.environ`. Then:
- `auth status --json` — backend, service/account, configured + `keyring|environment|missing`, NO values.
- `auth setup --dry-run --json` / `--yes --json` — getpass; validation-before-write; multi-entry rollback; no secrets in argv/stdin.
- `auth check --json`, `auth delete --dry-run --yes --json`.
- `doctor --json` — validates manifest/types/defaults, keyring backend, effective sources (no values); Confluence/model probes REPORTED `not_configured` (skipped) when creds missing; semantic exit codes (2 incomplete config, 4 rejected creds, 1 provider failure). Atomic, no prompts/writes.

### 3. OKF writer + validator
Frontmatter (SPEC §4.1 + producer keys):
```yaml
---
type: Note            # REQUIRED, non-empty
title: "..."
description: "..."    # stub: first heading/line; Task 2 = LLM
resource: "..."       # source URI/id or absent
tags: []              # stub empty; Task 2 = LLM
timestamp: 2026-07-18T00:00:00Z
source: note          # producer key
source_id: <stable id>
content_hash: <sha256>
okf_version: "0.1"
---
```
- Emit via `yaml.safe_dump(sort_keys=False, allow_unicode=True)`; CRITICAL: titles/tags with special chars (`:`, quotes, Cyrillic) survive round-trip (assert parse→emit→parse equality incl. Unicode).
- Slug: NFC-normalize title/filename → keep alphanumerics (ASCII) and Unicode word chars; collapse separators; if result empty (non-Latin only, e.g. pure Cyrillic) → fall back to `unidecode`-transliteration, then to `concept-<sha1[:10]>`; append `-2`,`-3` on collision WITHIN the same source dir; concept path `<vault>/<source>/<slug>.md`.
- `index.md` generator (no frontmatter except bundle-root `okf_version: "0.1"`; grouped sections with descriptions from frontmatter).
- `log.md` appender (ISO `YYYY-MM-DD`, newest first).
- `bundle validate --json` — SPEC §9: every non-reserved `.md` has parseable frontmatter + non-empty `type`; reserved filenames follow §6/§7. Exit 0 conformant; report violations list, never crash on a bad file (collect all errors).

### 4. Connectors (mechanical, no network, no LLM)
- `note add "<text>" | --file <f> [--title T] --json` → `type: Note`.
- `doc ingest <FILE|DIR> [--recursive] --json` → `.md/.txt` only here (read directly via UTF-8 with `errors="replace"` fallback noted); each → `type: Document`. markitdown+office = later task.
- Enrichment hook: pluggable `enrich(concept)` returning the concept unchanged except `description` from first heading/line and `tags=[]` — clearly marked stub; Task 2 swaps in the LLM provider. Keeps the pipeline green with no model key.

### 5. FTS5 indexer + search
`.okf/index.db` (open with `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`, `PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL`; directory created on demand):
```sql
CREATE TABLE concepts(concept_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, type TEXT, title TEXT,
  description TEXT, resource TEXT, source TEXT, source_id TEXT,
  content_hash TEXT, mtime TEXT);
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE concepts_fts USING fts5(
  concept_id UNINDEXED, title, description, body, tags,
  content='', tokenize='unicode61 remove_diacritics 2');
CREATE TABLE tags(tag TEXT, concept_id TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(tag, concept_id));
```
(FTS5 + `unicode61 remove_diacritics 2` verified available on system Python.)
- `bundle index [--rebuild|--since <ts>] --json` — incremental keyed on `source_id+content_hash`; on change: delete+reinsert FTS row, replace tags rows; idempotent (re-ingest same hash = no-op, no duplicate).
- `bundle stats --json` — counts by type/source.
- `search "<q>" [--tag T] [--type T] [--limit N] --json` — FTS5 `MATCH` with query chars escaped (`"`, `*`, `:`, `(`) → phrase query; intersect tag/type filters; `<100ms` for 1k concepts.
- `tag list --json`, `tag show <tag> --json`.
- Write `<vault>/.gitignore` containing `.okf/` on first write. NEVER `git init`/`git commit`.

### 6. Mutations + safety
`note add`, `doc ingest`, `bundle index`, `auth setup/delete` → `--dry-run --json` then `--yes --json`; show `target.path`. `OKF_INDEX_AUTO_CONFIRM=1` skips confirm (for evals).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `uv run --script scripts/run.py schema get --json` returns valid schema for all implemented commands; `--help` shows current time at top/resource/leaf.
- [ ] `doctor --json` runs with semantic exit codes; `rg -i "key|token|password" <stdout>` finds no secret VALUES (only account names).
- [ ] `note add "x"` and `doc ingest file.md` each write an OKF-conformant concept (parseable frontmatter + non-empty `type`) into `OKF_VAULT_PATH`; `bundle validate --json` exits 0.
- [ ] `bundle index --json` builds FTS5; `search "q" --tag t --type t --limit 5 --json` returns ranked bounded results in <100ms (1k fixture); `tag list/show` work.
- [ ] Incremental reindex: edit one fixture file → `bundle index --since` updates ONLY that concept (assert others' mtime unchanged in DB).
- [ ] Re-ingest of unchanged source (same `content_hash`) produces NO duplicate concept and NO duplicate FTS row.
- [ ] `<vault>/.gitignore` contains `.okf/`; no `git init`/`git commit` attempted (assert no `.git` touched).
- [ ] `auth status` prints zero secret values; non-secret env override works; credentials have no default in manifest.
- [ ] `python3 -m pytest` green; `python3 -m py_compile scripts/run.py` green; unknown command AND unknown flag both exit `2` with a stderr hint.
- [ ] `python3 -m json.tool evals/evals.json` valid.

## Key Considerations (SRE review)

**Unicode / Cyrillic (CRITICAL — content is Russian):**
- Titles/tags are UTF-8 with Cyrillic; NFC-normalize before slug/FTS.
- FTS5 `unicode61 remove_diacritics 2` indexes Cyrillic; queries MUST escape FTS5 special chars (`"`,`*`,`:`,`(`,`)`) → wrap as phrase `"q"` to avoid syntax errors / unintended column filters.
- YAML emit must use `allow_unicode=True`; never ASCII-escape Cyrillic in files (Obsidian readability).
- Test: pure-Cyrillic title slug → deterministic, non-empty, unique; round-trip preserves Cyrillic exactly.

**Slug collisions & idempotency:**
- Same title from different sources lives in different `<source>/` dirs (no collision across sources).
- Same title within one source dir → `-2`, `-3` suffix.
- Re-ingest of identical content (`content_hash` unchanged) = update-in-place / skip, NEVER a duplicate file or FTS row.
- Moving a source file between runs: `source_id` must be stable across path moves (derive from content+logical id, not absolute path) so reindex does not orphan rows.

**SQLite concurrency / durability:**
- WAL mode + short transactions; single writer. If a second process holds a write lock → return structured error `code:"busy"` (exit 1) with hint, do not hang.
- `.okf/` dir and `index.db` created lazily; missing dir is not an error (create), but a NON-writable vault path is exit 1 with a clear message.

**Malformed input / resilience:**
- `.md` without frontmatter, frontmatter not YAML, `type` empty/missing → `bundle validate` reports it, does not raise.
- `note add ""` (empty) → usage error exit 2.
- Huge file (>5 MB) → index body truncated to a bounded prefix with a `[truncated]` marker (avoid loading giant blobs into FTS); cap configurable later.
- Tags containing newlines/quotes → sanitized to single-line; never inject raw user text into YAML/SQL (parameterized inserts, `yaml.safe_dump`).
- Broken/relative cross-links in source markdown → preserved as-is (OKF §5.3 tolerates them).

**Vault safety:**
- Reject `OKF_VAULT_PATH` pointing inside the skill dir (prevent writing generated docs into code) — exit 2 with hint.
- `~` expansion and relative path resolution; create vault dir if missing and parent is writable.

**Determinism for tests:**
- Clock injected (no real time in JSON stdout / DB-derived assertions); `--help` time tested via injected clock only.
- `content_hash` = sha256 of normalized content (NFC, `\r\n`→`\n`).

## Anti-patterns (task-specific)
- ❌ NO references/imports/subprocess/hardcoded paths to external example skills (`.../AI/skills` or any sibling). Borrow logic by COPYING it into `okf-index`; the skill must run standalone.
- ❌ NO bare `except:` / `except Exception:` that swallows errors silently — catch specific exceptions, emit structured stderr error + exit code.
- ❌ NO assertions used as runtime validation (assert is for tests only).
- ❌ NO raw f-strings/`%` into SQL — parameterized statements only (FTS5 MATCH string is the exception and MUST be escaped).
- ❌ NO `os.environ[cred] = value` (credentials never copied to env).
- ❌ NO secret values in stdout/stderr/logs/argv/stdin.
- ❌ NO ASCII-escaping of Cyrillic in written files.
- ❌ NO `git init`/`git add`/`git commit` from the skill.
- ❌ NO direct `requests`/`urllib` HTTP in this task (no network needed).
- ❌ NO enrich stub pretending to be real LLM output (must be visibly a stub; real enrich = Task 2).

## Tests (named, each catches a real bug)
- `test_concept_without_type_is_rejected_by_validate`
- `test_concept_with_unparseable_frontmatter_reported_not_raised`
- `test_frontmatter_roundtrip_preserves_cyrillic_and_colons`
- `test_slug_pure_cyrillic_falls_back_deterministically`
- `test_slug_collision_within_source_suffixes`
- `test_reingest_same_content_hash_no_duplicate_file_or_fts_row`
- `test_source_id_stable_across_path_move`
- `test_fts5_query_with_special_chars_escaped` (`"`, `*`, `:`)
- `test_search_cyrillic_matches_unicode61_index`
- `test_search_tag_and_type_filters_intersect`
- `test_search_limit_bounds_results`
- `test_bundle_index_since_updates_only_changed_concept`
- `test_doctor_redacts_secret_values`
- `test_settings_env_overrides_nonsecret_default_only`
- `test_auth_status_never_prints_secrets` (fake keyring backend)
- `test_auth_setup_rolls_back_on_second_entry_failure` (fake keyring)
- `test_unknown_command_exits_2`, `test_unknown_flag_exits_2_with_hint`
- `test_vault_inside_skill_dir_rejected_exit_2`
- `test_nonwritable_vault_path_exits_1`
- `test_help_shows_injected_clock_not_real_time`
- `test_json_envelope_success_stdout_error_stderr`

## Evals
`evals/evals.json` — atomic evals (auto-confirm via `OKF_INDEX_AUTO_CONFIRM=1`, `eval-...` names, cleanup): `schema get`, `doctor`, `note add`, `doc ingest` (md), `bundle validate`, `bundle index`, `bundle stats`, `search`, `tag list`, `auth status`. Mutation evals create then remove their `eval-...` concept.
design:
## Goal
COORDINATOR. Decompose the okf-index core (Task 1) into 13 atomic SCIU subtasks, each a single TDD cycle + one commit. Track sequence; all must close for Task 1 to be complete. The detailed TDD plan + Stateless Handoff live in each child's design.

## SCIU Sequence (execute in order)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.1 — SCIU 1: CLI scaffold + schema get + JSON envelope + exit codes
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.2 — SCIU 2: help-with-time (injectable clock)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.3 — SCIU 3: settings.json manifest + loader
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.4 — SCIU 4: auth status (redacted)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.5 — SCIU 5: auth setup/check/delete (dry-run/--yes, rollback)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.6 — SCIU 6: doctor --json (atomic, exit codes)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.7 — SCIU 7: OKF concept model (frontmatter + slug + source_id)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.8 — SCIU 8: index.md + log.md generators
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.9 — SCIU 9: bundle validate (SPEC §9)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.10 — SCIU 10: connectors note add + doc ingest(md/txt) + enrich stub
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.11 — SCIU 11: FTS5 indexer (.okf/index.db, incremental, idempotent)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.12 — SCIU 12: search + tag list/show + stats (FTS escaping, Cyrillic)
- [ ] br-okf-index-kn0.1.13 — SCIU 13: evals + references + SKILL finalize + logo

## Dependencies
Linear chain via blocking deps (.2 depends on .1, ... .13 on .12). The auth block (4-6) runs after settings (3); connectors (10) after validator (9); indexer (11) after connectors; search (12) after indexer.

## Success Criteria (coordinator)

description:

- NO ASCII-escaping of Cyrillic in written files (allow_unicode=True).
- NO auto-init/auto-commit of git by the skill.
- NO enrich stub pretending to be real LLM output (must be visibly a stub until the enrich task).

## Goal
SCIU 1: CLI scaffold + schema get + JSON envelope + exit codes. One TDD cycle, one commit. Part of Task 1 (okf-index core) — see parent br-okf-index-kn0.1 for full context.

## Files
Create: okf-index/SKILL.md (stub), okf-index/scripts/run.py (PEP 723 header `# /// script` requires-python>=3.11, deps=[] for now), okf-index/scripts/envelope.py, okf-index/scripts/errors.py. okf-index/logo.svg.

## Tests (TDD — write failing first; each catches a real bug)
- test_schema_get_returns_valid_json (catches missing schema command)
- test_unknown_command_exits_2 (usage error)

envelope.py:
  def emit_success(data, code=0): json.dump({"success":True,"data":data}, sys.stdout); return 0
  def emit_error(code,message,hint=None,retriable=False,exit_code=1): json.dump({"success":False,"error":{"code":code,"message":message,"hint":hint,"retriable":retriable}}, sys.stderr); return exit_code
errors.py: class SkillError(Exception) with .code .retriable .exit_code; UsageError(exit_code=2); NotFoundError(3); PermissionError(4); ConflictError(5).

- [ ] unknown flag -> exit 2 + stderr hint

cd okf-index && uv run --script scripts/run.py schema get --json

## Implementation sketch (real shapes, not placeholders)
db.py: open <vault>/.okf/index.db; PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL; create if missing.
ftschema.py:
  CREATE TABLE concepts(concept_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, type, title, description, resource, source, source_id, content_hash, mtime);
  CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE concepts_fts USING fts5(concept_id UNINDEXED, title, description, body, tags, content='', tokenize='unicode61 remove_diacritics 2');
  CREATE TABLE tags(tag TEXT, concept_id TEXT, PRIMARY KEY(tag, concept_id));
hash.py: content_hash (reuse concept.content_hash).
bundle index [--rebuild|--since ts]: scan concepts; for changed (content_hash or mtime) -> delete+reinsert fts row, replace tags; idempotent (unchanged = no-op, no dup).

- [ ] index builds with WAL; re-ingest of unchanged content creates no duplicate; --since touches only changed concepts; moved source updates in place
